subject: Human Resources
title: RSUs and Restricted Stock
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: RSUs and Restricted Stock.

hook.text: A tech worker's RSU grant vests over four years. Each year, shares deposit like clockwork—real money, not promises.

buildup.text: RSUs—Restricted Stock Units—are company shares granted to employees that vest over time, usually 3-4 years.

discovery.text: Unlike options, RSUs always have value as long as the stock price is above zero—making them lower risk.

twist.text: RSUs are taxed as ordinary income when they vest—creating surprise tax bills that catch employees off guard.

climax.text: Large tech companies use RSUs as a major retention tool—golden handcuffs that keep talent through vesting cycles.

punchline.text: RSUs are real shares with real value. And real taxes.

quiz.question: How do RSUs differ from stock options?
options: RSUs have value as long as the stock price is above zero
